TSLA: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 04/27/2025. This article lists the top 10 performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending April 27, 2025, with returns ranging from 18.05% to 21.95%. Key drivers for these performances include caution around growth, tariffs, trade uncertainty, big tech earnings, Tesla's sales drop, increased market volatility, U.S. export restrictions on AI chips, and safe-haven demand for gold. The article details specific ETFs like SSG, TSLQ, UVIX, and NVDS, explaining the factors contributing to their strong weekly gains.

Forget Tesla: This 1 Auto Stock Is an Outperformer in 2025. While Tesla faces challenges in 2025 due to slowing deliveries, political distractions, and competition, Ferrari N.V. (RACE) is highlighted as an outperformer. Ferrari's strong demand from affluent clientele, robust guidance, and upcoming EV launch position it for continued success despite economic headwinds and tariffs. Analysts widely recommend Ferrari as a "Moderate Buy" with significant upside potential.

Tesla hiring over 1,000 workers to ramp up Semi truck production, Business Insider reports. Tesla plans to hire over 1,000 workers in Nevada to increase the mass production of its Semi electric trucks, as reported by Business Insider. The company recently announced that initial production of the Semi trucks will begin at the Gigafactory in Sparks, Nevada, by the end of 2025, aiming for an annual capacity of 50,000 units. This move signifies a significant ramp-up in Tesla's long-delayed truck-making business, which initially aimed for 2019 production.

MercadoLibre proposes to move US legal home to Texas from Delaware. MercadoLibre's board has asked shareholders to approve moving its U.S. operations from Delaware to Texas, citing a desire for greater legal certainty. This move, echoing similar actions by companies like Tesla, is scheduled for a shareholder vote on June 17, and if successful, could benefit MercadoLibre while posing a challenge to Asian e-commerce competitors. The company believes Texas's recently enacted rule for codifying business judgments will provide more predictable decision-making for its innovative business model.

New PG&E Service Offering Makes It Easier and Faster to Connect EV Chargers, EV Fleets and Big Batteries to the Grid. PG&E has introduced "Flexible Service Connection" (Flex Connect) which uses a cloud-based Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) to allow EV fast-charging stations and grid-scale batteries to connect to the grid sooner, without waiting for physical infrastructure upgrades. This system coordinates power demand based on electricity supply availability, sending hourly power forecasts to customers. Flex Connect has already expedited connections for Tesla Supercharger sites and PepsiCo's EV semi-truck fleet, enabling earlier EV adoption and significant emissions and cost savings.

This Famous Investor Thinks Tesla's Stock Price Will Reach $2,600 Due to 1 Exciting Growth Catalyst. Cathie Wood, manager of the ARK Innovation ETF, believes Tesla's stock price could soar to $2,600 within five years, representing over 1,000% growth from its current $300 per share. Her optimistic forecast is primarily driven by the anticipated rollout of Tesla's robotaxi network and the new Cybercab model in 2026, which she expects to revolutionize autonomous ride-sharing. However, the author expresses skepticism, comparing Wood's predictions to Uber's current market cap and historical over-optimism.

Alphabet Sets Positive Tone Ahead of Tech Earnings. This article discusses the Q1 earnings season with a focus on big tech companies. While Tesla reported a decline in earnings and revenue, Alphabet surpassed analyst estimates due to strong performance in its search and advertising units, driven by AI investments. Intel also beat estimates but provided weak forward-looking guidance and announced workforce reductions.

TESL Stock Price, News & Analysis. Simplify Volt TSLA Revolution ETF (TESL) stock has seen a 19.9% decline over the past year, with shares last trading at $13.41. The company's short interest has decreased significantly, suggesting limited bearish sentiment, and its low days-to-cover ratio indicates high liquidity. The article provides a comprehensive overview of TESL, including performance data, news, and financial highlights.

Elon Musk’s Tesla quietly left off US$97m Bitcoin loss in earnings report. Tesla reportedly excluded a US$97 million Bitcoin-related loss from its adjusted earnings in its latest report, which boosted its non-GAAP earnings by about 12 percent. This accounting practice helped present a stronger financial picture, offsetting what was otherwise Tesla's weakest quarterly performance in years. This inconsistency in how crypto gains and losses are treated in non-GAAP earnings could attract scrutiny from the SEC, which has previously reprimanded companies for similar practices.
SBUX: Why Starbucks Stock Fell 18% in April. Starbucks (SBUX) stock dropped 18% in April due to President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs announcement and an underwhelming fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Despite management's claims of a recovery strategy, the company missed revenue and earnings estimates, with comparable sales and adjusted operating margin declining. CEO Brian Niccol aims to turn the business around amidst macro headwinds.

April earnings update: Robinhood, Sleep Number, Starbucks, Ally Financial and State Street. This article compiles insights from CFOs during their April earnings calls, highlighting strategic moves, financial performance, and future outlooks for several companies. Key discussions include Robinhood's strong retail investor engagement, Sleep Number's cost reduction plan, Starbucks' turnaround strategy, and Ally Financial's efforts to boost net interest margin. Other companies like Independent Bank, State Street, First Industrial Realty Trust, Fastenal, and Guaranty Bancshares also shared updates on competitive markets, expense flexibility, debt restructuring, and loan loss reserves.

Starbucks outlook shifts to negative, Moody’s affirms ratings. Moody's Ratings has changed Starbucks' outlook from stable to negative due to weakening profitability and credit metrics, stemming from sales deleveraging and increased labor investments as part of its "Back to Starbucks" reinvention plan. Despite the negative outlook, Moody's affirmed all of Starbucks' ratings, including Baa1 senior unsecured notes and Prime-2 commercial paper program, citing the company's strong brand, market position, and liquidity. However, challenges like heightened competition, cost pressures, and consumer spending uncertainty could lead to a downgrade if comparable sales weaken or financial policies become more aggressive.

Circular Action Alliance adds to founding members. The Circular Action Alliance (CAA), a nonprofit producer responsibility organization, has announced The Campbell’s Co., Ikea Supply AG, and Starbucks Corp. as new founding members. These companies will also gain representation on CAA's board, strengthening its governance as it implements extended producer responsibility (EPR) laws for packaging in various U.S. states. CAA operates as a PRO in California, Colorado, Oregon, and Minnesota, playing a critical role in representing producer interests in EPR programs.

Starbucks Profit Drops, but Leaders Say Turnaround Is Working. Starbucks reported a 50 percent drop in quarterly profit, but its CEO, Brian Niccol, asserted that the company's turnaround strategy is showing progress. Despite challenges like higher coffee prices and tariffs, global same-store sales improved, and the company is implementing initiatives like hiring more baristas and testing order-sequencing programs to enhance customer experience. The profit decline was partly attributed to investments in additional workers for the turnaround and restructuring costs.

Starbucks union rejects company's recent offer of at least 2% annual pay raise. Starbucks union delegates have rejected the company's latest contract proposal, which included a guaranteed annual raise of at least 2% but no changes to economic benefits or immediate pay hikes. Workers United stated that 81% of the 490 baristas representing over 550 unionized U.S. stores voted against the offer, deeming it insufficient. Starbucks criticized the union for delaying a mutual agreement by presenting an incomplete framework to its delegates.

Starbucks Union Says It Rejects Company’s Proposal for 2% Annual Raise Guarantee. Starbucks union delegates have rejected the company's latest contract proposal, which offered a guaranteed annual raise of at least 2%. The Workers United union announced that 81% of delegates from over 550 unionized US stores voted against the proposal, deeming it insufficient. This decision highlights ongoing tensions in contract negotiations between Starbucks and its unionized workforce.
DIS: Brand Creates Exclusive Collectible Cans in celebration of Disney’s “Lilo & Stitch” Live-Action Movie. The SPAM® brand has announced a collaboration with Disney's upcoming live-action "Lilo & Stitch" movie, releasing exclusive Hawaiian-inspired limited-edition collectible cans featuring artwork from the film. This partnership also includes a new TV commercial campaign starting May 5, celebrating the cultural connection both the SPAM® brand and Stitch share with Hawaiian traditions and family. The collectible cans are available nationwide, with the movie set to premiere in theaters on May 23, 2025.

The Walt Disney Company To Participate In The Moffettnathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The Walt Disney Company announced that Josh D’Amaro, Chairman, Disney Experiences, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The session is scheduled for Wednesday, May 14, 2025, at approximately 9:40 a.m. ET/ 6:40 a.m. PT. A live stream will be available on the company's investor relations website, where an archived recording will also be accessible.

The Walt Disney Company Launches Inaugural, Company-Wide “Disney Week Of Wishes” Campaign To Celebrate World Wish Day With Make-A-Wish. The Walt Disney Company has launched its first-ever "Disney Week of Wishes" campaign to celebrate World Wish Day on April 29 and its 45-year partnership with Make-A-Wish. This week-long event highlights Disney's unique ability to grant wishes, with special moments like a Princess-themed ball, meeting Marvel stars, and a wish-granting moment on American Idol. Disney, the world's largest wish granter for Make-A-Wish, aims to bring happiness and hope to children with critical illnesses globally, having granted over 165,000 wishes since 1981.

Jim Cramer Says The Walt Disney Company (DIS) ‘Just Has Been A One-way Trip To The Danger Zone Since Nelson Peltz Was No Longer Involved’. Jim Cramer commented on The Walt Disney Company (DIS), stating that the stock has been in a "danger zone" since activist investor Nelson Peltz was no longer involved. He expressed a prior belief that Peltz's presence on the board would have fostered operational discipline, a prediction he felt was validated by the stock's subsequent decline. Cramer also shared insights on the broader market, attributing a rally to President Trump's clarification that he would not fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell, and discussed the evolving perception of market recoveries.
